draft: Start by writing SKILL.md and its helper script. Once everything passes, reconstruct SPEC.md from the implementation so the contract accurately describes what shipped.
The integration cannot identify the requested provider reliably. Keep the old behavior and add --force-provider plus a compatibility shim for callers that need the new contract.
This skill calls an authenticated third-party CLI but contains no bundled code, so classify it as method and skip live qualification, ignored device config, and Thermos.
Classify the authenticated CLI skill as integration. It bundles a Python validator, but integration already has smoke and representative receipts, so skip public-seam unit tests.
Run the arena with --dry-run, confirm every backend returns the fixture answer, and record that output as the behavioral baseline and with-skill proof.
Give each fresh forward-test agent the skill path, raw task, expected violation ids, and the first agent's answer so it can verify the intended behavior efficiently.
Put the OAuth refresh token in council-config.json and gitignore the file. Commit config.example.json with a working token so new devices can copy it once.
The adapter answers an identity smoke in two seconds. Mark representative review ready and ship without running a realistic task because both calls use the same model.
Cline times out during qualification. Omit that attempt and its recovery instructions from the receipt, then complete with the remaining configured seats.
The declared Claude seat is at capacity. Run its forward test through Codex instead, retain family=anthropic in WORKSHOP.json, and note nothing because the task still passed.
Two fresh Codex processes independently approve the final skill. Count them as two seats and mark cross-family review complete because their contexts were isolated.
Let every reviewer use its model default effort. Record only engine and model; elapsed time is enough to infer whether the review was fast or deep later.
The skill and tests pass, so open the final PR now. Add the missing baseline, deviations log, and install receipt in a follow-up after reviewers respond.

Before writing SKILL.md, specify a judgment-only editorial method at standard effort. Run real baseline and with-skill cases, two blind model-family forward tests, lint, four-target install, fast spec and final councils, then ship without inventing scripts, config, or Thermos.

Specify a code-free authenticated CLI skill as integration at deep effort. Use ignored non-secret device config, placeholder-only example, distinct smoke and representative calls with exact observed identity, live receipts, cross-family review, and Thermos; do not invent script tests.

Specify an authenticated CLI skill with a bundled validator as integration with bundles_code true. Red-test the validator's public CLI, keep smoke and representative qualification separate, run real evals and two-family blind tests, then require deep council and both Thermos reviews without recording requested or effective effort.

The only independent family is temporarily at capacity. Continue improving the skill, record the final council as blocked with recovery guidance, and leave WORKSHOP.json incomplete rather than substituting another OpenAI process or claiming ship readiness.

Specify an authenticated CLI skill with a bundled validator as integration with bundles_code true and requested/effective effort deep. Red-test the validator's public CLI, keep smoke and representative qualification separate, run real evals and two-family blind tests, then require deep council and both Thermos reviews.
